name: Story DoR — SF Origination Extensions (Starter Draft)
description: Placeholder criteria for Single Family Origination stories. SF Origination stewards should review and customize.
placeholder_note: Starter content. Replace with real SF Origination DoR criteria.

criteria.name: AUS Rule Version Stated
criteria.description: Which version of the Automated Underwriting System rule set the story applies against is named.
criteria.why: AUS rule sets evolve; building against an unnamed version causes silent regression as rules update.
criteria.fix: State the AUS version (e.g. DU 11.0, LPA 24.2) and the date it became effective for this story.
criteria.good_example: AUS: DU 11.0 (effective 2026-04-15). Tested against DU sandbox on 2026-04-30.

criteria.name: TILA/RESPA Disclosure Impact Identified
criteria.description: Whether the change touches TILA/RESPA-regulated disclosure content (LE, CD, NOIR) is stated.
criteria.why: Disclosure changes can trigger 3-day or 7-day re-disclosure timing rules — silent changes are a UDAAP risk.
criteria.fix: State whether TILA/RESPA disclosures are touched. If yes, name the compliance review owner.
criteria.good_example: Touches Loan Estimate (Section A: Origination Charges). Compliance review by [name] on [date].

criteria.name: Lender Integration Contract Reviewed
criteria.description: If the story affects a lender-facing interface (API, portal, file format), the integration contract is reviewed and downstream lenders are notified.
criteria.why: Lender systems integrate against published contracts; silent breaks cause same-day production incidents.
criteria.fix: Confirm the integration contract is unchanged or that breaking changes have been communicated to lenders.
criteria.good_example: AUS Decision API contract unchanged (v3.2). Or: 'Adds new optional field decision_subcode; backward-compatible. Lender bulletin sent 2026-04-25.'

criteria.name: Borrower-Facing Copy Approved
criteria.description: If the story produces text seen by borrowers (emails, letters, portal screens), the copy is approved by Marketing/Compliance.
criteria.why: Borrower-facing copy is a regulatory and brand surface — unapproved text creates UDAAP and brand risk.
criteria.fix: Confirm copy approval from Marketing/Compliance. Or attest 'no borrower-facing copy in this story.'
criteria.good_example: Email subject and body approved by Marketing on 2026-05-01. Or: 'No borrower-facing copy in this story.'
